author: Авакумов Данил Эдуардович
objective: Determine whether emergent cooperative architecture depends on task information structure rather than algorithmic parameters.

key_findings: Architecture invariant across selection scaling, weight injection, and evaluation-noise regimes.
Empirical weight law strongly describes equilibrium but does not control dynamics.
background.working_hypothesis: Emergent cooperative architecture is determined by task information structure rather than algorithm parameters.

research_hypothesis.statement: Different task information structures produce different cooperative role architectures.

task_families.description: Tasks where direct prediction contains most information (baseline family).
Tasks where all roles contribute equally to solution quality.
Tasks where error detection dominates solution improvement.
Tasks requiring integration of multiple weak signals.

experiment_blocks.title: TASK FAMILY IMPLEMENTATION
experiment_blocks.goal: Implement synthetic task families with controlled information structures.

experiment_blocks.title: TASK FAMILY EXECUTION
experiment_blocks.goal: Run coevolution experiments for each task family.

experiment_blocks.title: ROLE ARCHITECTURE EXTRACTION
experiment_blocks.goal: Compute emergent role ratios for each task family.

experiment_blocks.title: ARCHITECTURE COMPARISON
experiment_blocks.goal: Compare role architectures across task families.

experiment_blocks.title: ARCHITECTURE CLUSTERING
experiment_blocks.goal: Cluster cooperative architectures by task structure.

experiment_blocks.title: TASK-ARCHITECTURE MAP
experiment_blocks.goal: Construct mapping between task structure and cooperative architecture.

experiment_blocks.title: FINAL REPORT
experiment_blocks.goal: Document relationship between task information structure and emergent cooperative architecture.

definition_of_done: Experiments executed for each task family.
